---
title: "Customer Visa Status Analysis"
author: "Data Analyst Student"
date: "2024-04-19"
description: "An analysis of fictional travel agency visa statuses using Pandas and Matplotlib."
categories: [Python, Data Visualization, Matplotlib]
---

## Introduction

This project demonstrates my ability to clean data and generate visualizations using Python. We generate a mock dataset simulating customer visa approvals for a travel agency, and then plot the results.

## Data Generation

We'll use `pandas` and `matplotlib` for this analysis.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Generate mock data
np.random.seed(42)
categories = ['Approved', 'Pending', 'Rejected', 'More Info Required']
data = np.random.choice(categories, 100, p=[0.6, 0.2, 0.1, 0.1])

df = pd.DataFrame({'Visa_Status': data})
status_counts = df['Visa_Status'].value_counts()

print("Raw Data Sample:")
print(df.head())
```

## Visualization

Next, we render a bar chart to visualize the distribution of visa statuses. Quarto automatically executes this code chunk and embeds the resulting plot directly in the output document.

```{python}
#| label: fig-visastatus
#| fig-cap: Distribution of Visa Statuses

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
status_counts.plot(kind='bar', color=['#27ae60', '#f39c12', '#e74c3c', '#3498db'])
plt.title('Customer Visa Status Overview', fontsize=14)
plt.ylabel('Number of Applicants')
plt.xlabel('Status')
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.7)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Observations
As we can see from @fig-visastatus, the vast majority of applications are **Approved**, which indicates the travel agency has a robust documentation process prior to submission.

## Advanced Markdown: Mermaid Graph
Here is a flowchart representing the typical customer pipeline:
```{mermaid}
graph LR
  A[Inquiry] --> B[Booking Started]
  B --> C[Payments Received]
  C --> D[Visa Processing]
  D --> E[Docs Handover]
```
